name: San Onofre / Old Man’s / Dogpatch
direction: rights and lefts; soft, slow, slopey
bestSeason: summer–fall for longboard-friendly S/SW swells; winter can be cleaner/consistent
skillNotes: Classic mellow longboard zone; Dogpatch is especially soft/beginner-friendly; crowded but generally forgiving.
whyItBelongs: One of California’s defining longboard waves: trim, glide, noseride-friendly shoulders.

name: Malibu Surfrider
skillLevel: intermediate to advanced because of crowd/positioning
skillNotes: Technically perfect but extremely crowded and competitive; etiquette is critical.
whyItBelongs: Iconic long right walls, trim sections, and noseride potential; a global longboard benchmark.

name: Rincon
skillNotes: World-class and sectiony; positioning, current, rocks, crowd pressure, and speed matter.
whyItBelongs: Long point-wave flow with high-line trimming and classic California longboard history.

name: Cardiff Reef
skillNotes: Reef and crowd awareness needed; softer on smaller days, more serious with size.
whyItBelongs: Long, open reef shoulders with trim-friendly walls and strong longboard culture.

name: Pleasure Point / Cowells
breakType: reef/point setup; Cowells is a sheltered soft point
direction: mostly rights at Pleasure Point; Cowells has soft rights/inside rollers
bestSeason: fall–winter; Cowells best when enough west swell wraps in
skillLevel: beginner-friendly at Cowells; intermediate at Pleasure Point
skillNotes: Cowells is beginner/longboard friendly but crowded; Pleasure Point has more reef, current, and local crowd pressure.
whyItBelongs: Santa Cruz longboard staple with long rights, playful sections, and historic surf culture.

name: Church
skillNotes: Softer than Lowers, but still has rocks and crowd pressure; good for midlengths and longboards.
whyItBelongs: More forgiving Trestles-area wave with long, lined-up shoulders on south swell.

name: C Street
skillNotes: Long and rippable but crowded; current, rocks, and stronger winter surf require experience.
whyItBelongs: Long right walls and multiple takeoff zones make it a strong longboard/midlength point-wave entry.

name: Blackies
direction: mostly lefts with point influence; mixed peaks depending sand
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on small days
skillNotes: Friendly longboard scene but can be crowded; rips and winter punch need respect.
whyItBelongs: Newport’s classic small-wave longboard hangout with soft, easy rollers when modest.

name: Swami’s
skillNotes: Quality wave with rocks and heavy crowd/local presence; not ideal for true beginners.
whyItBelongs: Long, clean reef rights with excellent trim and turn sections for skilled longboarders.

name: Doheny
skillNotes: Soft, slow, very approachable; crowded with learners and longboards; watch rocks and water quality after rain.
whyItBelongs: Gentle peelers and forgiving takeoffs make it one of SoCal’s best beginner longboard waves.

name: Tourmaline
bestSeason: summer–fall S/SW swells; winter can be consistent
skillNotes: Classic mellow longboard and fish wave; crowded but generally less high-performance than nearby reefs.
whyItBelongs: San Diego’s archetypal longboard playground: soft peaks, easy takeoffs, trim sections.

name: Mondos
skillNotes: Very mellow learning/longboard wave; can be crowded when small and clean.
whyItBelongs: Soft, slow rollers near Ventura that suit gliding, learners, and relaxed longboard sessions.

name: Kitty Hawk Pier
bestSeason: summer for consistency; fall for cleaner tropical/groundswell windows
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on small days
skillNotes: Public OBX pier zone; can get crowded, with rips and pier hazards.
whyItBelongs: Reliable, accessible Outer Banks beachbreak with rideable left/right sandbar peaks.

name: Jennette’s Pier
skillNotes: Very public, easy-access Nags Head spot; watch pier/current zones.
whyItBelongs: Recognizable public OBX setup; sandbars can produce forgiving, makeable shoulders.

name: Kill Devil Hills / Avalon Pier zone
bestSeason: fall–winter; small summer windswell can be longboardable
skillNotes: Classic public OBX town beach feel; crowds possible near better sandbars and piers.
whyItBelongs: Realistic longboard seed candidate when sandbars line up.

name: S-Turns / Rodanthe
skillNotes: More exposed OBX energy; currents, shifting bars, and crowd pressure when good.
whyItBelongs: Publicly known Hatteras Island sandbar zone with peeling potential when small-to-medium and organized.

name: Avon Pier
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on smaller days
skillNotes: Public pier zone; more manageable than heavier Hatteras spots when small, but still exposed to OBX currents.
whyItBelongs: Known Hatteras Island pier setup that can offer long, softer sandbar shoulders.

name: Old Lighthouse / Buxton
skillNotes: Not a mellow beginner spot with size; small, clean, lined-up days can peel.
whyItBelongs: One of NC’s iconic public surf zones with strong sandbar potential.

name: Frisco Pier / Frisco
breakType: beach / pier break, somewhat more sheltered depending wind/swell
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on small days
skillNotes: Public Hatteras south-facing zone; watch shifting sand and structure/current context.
whyItBelongs: Often a mellower Hatteras option for clean, smaller longboard runners.

name: Wrightsville Beach / Oceanic–Crystal–Mercer pier zones
direction: mixed peaks; often lefts/rights depending bar
skillNotes: Highly accessible surf-town vibe, popular/crowded, with lots of learners and longboarders.
whyItBelongs: One of the obvious southern NC longboard seeds: public, consistent enough, established surf community.

name: Masonboro Island / Masonboro Inlet
skillNotes: Access is less casual than town beaches; boat/kayak/paddle logistics and changing inlet currents matter.
whyItBelongs: Publicly listed NC beachbreak with right/left waves and less dense crowd than central Wrightsville.

name: Surf City Pier / Topsail Island
bestSeason: fall–winter; small summer windswells for logging
skillNotes: Public pier-town setup; family beach vibe, can get busy around the pier.
whyItBelongs: Realistic southern/central NC longboard card: softer beachbreak peaks, public access, small-wave days.

name: The Washout, Folly Beach
bestSeason: fall–winter; tropical season can produce quality windows
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on small days; intermediate when bigger/crowded
skillNotes: Public Charleston-area surf zone; busy and variable.
whyItBelongs: Strong nearby East Coast addition with friendly sandbar shoulders in clean smaller surf.

name: Cocoa Beach Pier / Cocoa Beach
bestSeason: winter and hurricane season; summer often small but longboardable
skillNotes: Very public, established surf/longboard/learner scene; crowds common but approachable.
whyItBelongs: Archetypal East Coast longboard-friendly town wave: soft, accessible, public, small-wave famous.

name: Waikiki / Queens / Canoes
direction: mostly long rights with soft lefts/inside reform sections
bestSeason: summer south swells; small clean days year-round
skillNotes: Iconic but crowded; reef, rental traffic, canoes, and etiquette matter even when the wave is soft.
whyItBelongs: Probably the global symbol of easy longboard glide: warm water, long soft walls, and historic surf culture.

name: Waikiki / Publics
skillNotes: More reef awareness than Canoes/Queens; still public and longboard-friendly on smaller days.
whyItBelongs: A Waikiki longboard extension with playful soft shoulders and a little more room to explore.

name: Honoliʻi
skillNotes: Rocky entry, rain/runoff, current, and local etiquette matter; not a beginner-only setup.
whyItBelongs: A lush Big Island classic with softer longboardable faces when small and organized.

name: Diamond Head Cliffs
skillNotes: Wind, reef, current, and walk-in access make it less casual than Waikiki proper.
whyItBelongs: A more open-feeling south shore option with longboardable faces on friendly days.

name: La Saladita
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on smaller days
skillNotes: Long paddle, crowds, rocks/reef awareness near takeoff, and etiquette matter.
whyItBelongs: One of the dream soft-left log waves: long, slow, trim-friendly, and built for glide.

name: Sayulita
bestSeason: winter–spring for cleaner consistency; summer south pulses
skillNotes: Very crowded with schools and rentals; water quality and etiquette can be issues.
whyItBelongs: A famous, accessible Mexican longboard town wave with soft takeoffs and small-wave consistency.

name: La Lancha
bestSeason: winter–spring; also summer south swell windows
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on smaller days
skillNotes: Reef, crowd, and access trail/boat logistics; more size raises the skill floor.
whyItBelongs: Warm-water glide with soft shoulders, jungle access, and classic Punta Mita longboard appeal.

name: San Blas / Stoner’s Point
skillNotes: Long wave but access, crowds, bugs, currents, and condition dependency make it less beginner-simple.
whyItBelongs: Legendary long right with classic trim-and-run potential when it lines up.

name: 9 Palms
location: East Cape, Baja California Sur, Mexico
skillNotes: Remote-ish access, reef/rocks, heat, wind, and limited services require planning.
whyItBelongs: A Baja dream-list right with mellow longboard sections on smaller south swells.

name: Cerritos
skillNotes: Popular learner/longboard beach; currents and size can change quickly.
whyItBelongs: Accessible Baja soft-wave hub with forgiving shoulders and a strong longboard/learner scene.

name: Scorpion Bay / San Juanico
skillNotes: Remote drive/logistics, rocks, crowds at better points, and long paddles; respect the town and lineup.
whyItBelongs: A lifetime longboard pilgrimage: stacked right points with glide for days when the swell fits.

name: Noosa Main Point
bestSeason: summer cyclone swells and autumn windows
skillNotes: Can be extremely crowded; national park points require etiquette and patience.
whyItBelongs: A world-class right point that looks designed for logging, trimming, and noseriding.

name: Byron Bay / The Pass
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on small days; crowd makes it harder
skillNotes: Very crowded with all craft and skill levels; etiquette is the main hazard.
whyItBelongs: A famous warm-water right with long easy walls and a full longboard dream-trip feel.

name: Crescent Head
skillNotes: Rocks, sweep, crowd, and long rides demand positioning and fitness.
whyItBelongs: A classic Australian longboard point with long trim walls and deep surf history.

name: Côte des Basques
bestSeason: summer–early autumn for friendlier longboard conditions
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on small days
skillNotes: Tide/access matters; crowds and shorebreak vary with conditions.
whyItBelongs: European longboard icon: beautiful setting, soft peaks, and deep surf history.

name: Zarautz
skillNotes: Open beach can be shifty and crowded; size and currents change the experience.
whyItBelongs: A public Basque longboard/learner hub with soft peaks on friendly days.

name: Batu Karas
skillNotes: Generally known as mellow, but reef/rocks, crowds, and travel context still matter.
whyItBelongs: A famously soft Indonesian right with longboard-friendly walls and warm-water travel appeal.

name: Medewi
skillNotes: Cobblestones, currents, long paddle, and size can be serious; smaller days suit logging better.
whyItBelongs: A long, mellow-ish left with huge trim potential when clean and manageable.

name: Weligama Bay
skillNotes: Busy learner zone; softer bay waves, board traffic, and schools are the main concerns.
whyItBelongs: Warm-water, soft, accessible longboard and learner bay with forgiving little runners.

name: Banana Point / Taghazout area
bestSeason: winter North Atlantic swells; smaller days for longboards
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on small days
skillNotes: Rocks, crowds, tide, and size matter; nearby points can get powerful.
whyItBelongs: A softer Taghazout-area right that can offer approachable trim sections in a broader point-wave zone.

name: Tamarindo
bestSeason: December–April cleaner dry season; May–November bigger south/west energy
skillNotes: Crowds, estuary currents, and crocodile warnings near river mouths require awareness.
whyItBelongs: Accessible warm-water surf town with plenty of soft longboardable days.

name: Playa Guiones / Nosara
bestSeason: December–April for cleaner smaller surf; green season for more size
skillNotes: Wide beach, rips, sun, and crowd zones; more size quickly raises difficulty.
whyItBelongs: Consistent, forgiving warm-water peaks and a longboard-friendly travel scene.

name: Pavones
skillNotes: Long rides, rocks, river/current, travel logistics, and crowds when firing.
whyItBelongs: A bucket-list left with huge longboard glide potential on smaller, cleaner south swells.

name: El Sunzal
bestSeason: March–October south swells; smaller days for logs
skillNotes: Rocks, long paddle, crowd, and size; not a beginner beach but can be forgiving when small.
whyItBelongs: A long, slopey right point with warm-water trim sections and classic travel appeal.

name: El Zonte
skillNotes: Rocks, currents, and size; smaller clean days are more log-friendly.
whyItBelongs: A well-known public right with mellow shoulders when conditions line up.

name: Playa Maderas
location: near San Juan del Sur, Nicaragua
bestSeason: November–April cleaner winds; south swells throughout much of year
skillNotes: Can get punchy/crowded; rips and rocks at edges; not every day is mellow.
whyItBelongs: A public Nicaragua travel staple with plenty of small, fun, longboardable windows.

name: Encuentro
bestSeason: winter north swells; morning glass-off windows
skillLevel: beginner-friendly to intermediate on small days
skillNotes: Reef, urchins, and crowd zones; choose the softer peaks and smaller days.
whyItBelongs: Caribbean warm-water surf hub with accessible longboard-friendly shoulders in the right conditions.
